methodology.aim: Scientific adequacy rather than computational tractability
methodology.problem: Likelihood computation becomes effectively impossible when moving away from Gaussian processes

approximate_methods.name: Likelihood-free inference
approximate_methods.reference: Grelaud et al., 2009; Gutmann and Corander, 2016

methodology.abc_approach: Avoids evaluating intractable likelihoods by matching summary statistics from observations with those computed from simulated data based on parameters drawn from a predefined prior distribution

abc_components.name: ε
abc_components.description: Trade-off between sample efficiency and inference quality

results.limitations: ABC is not scalable to large numbers of observations

solutions.method: Classifier to approximate density ratios proportional to likelihood estimation

solutions.limitation: Case-specific and not scalable, CNN must be retrained with new MLEs for each new testing dataset

solutions.benefit: Avoids the shortcoming of using MLEs
